candidates.title: Release-Maintenance Drift Hardening
candidates.description: Hardens the release-maintenance path so release-identity state stays metadata-driven. Adds a reverse-drift roadmap guard to scripts/check_public_docs_consistency.py (_released_candidate_ids and _check_autonomy_roadmap_released_candidates_not_next_planned) that flags an already-released candidate listed as a candidate-entry bullet under the next-planned planning line, plus deterministic tests. Retains the metadata-driven release-assurance demo default. Docs/checker/test-only: no runtime, safety, CLI, broker, provider, credential, version, or release-metadata behavior changes.
notes: Accepted into the v0.6.22 candidate chain on 2026-07-13 with acceptance verdict PASS.
The reverse-drift roadmap guard and its tests, plus the metadata-driven demo default, have landed on main as unreleased supporting work.
Metadata-driven: released candidate IDs and the next-planned tag are read from docs/releases/release-metadata.json, not pinned to a release number.
Directly addresses the release-maintenance drift observed after the v0.6.21 cutover (stale next-planned tag guard, stale demo default, roadmap candidate mislabeling).
Deliberate per-release version-pin tripwire assertions are intentionally left unchanged so they still fail at cutover to force review.
Does not enable live trading, live submit, order placement, broker/provider execution, credential loading, network access, or approval queue mutation.
Does not change RiskManager, kill-switch, deadman, heartbeat, or audit hash-chain behavior, and does not broaden the CAND-014 extraction boundary.
No PyPI publication; no tag or GitHub Release. v0.6.22 remains the next planned release line and is not released.
